prompt: The reflection call throws NoSuchMethodException. What is the bug?
choices: getMethod() cannot find int parameters — use getDeclaredMethod() with Integer.class
getMethod() only finds public methods; add() is package-private, so getDeclaredMethod() must be used
Method.invoke() cannot accept int literals — use Integer objects
int.class is not a valid class literal for method lookup
explanation: getMethod() finds only public methods inherited or declared. add() has package-private access (no modifier), so getMethod() throws NoSuchMethodException. Fix: use getDeclaredMethod("add", int.class, int.class) which finds all declared methods regardless of access. int.class is the correct class literal for primitive int.
